key_insights.insight: Prioritize scheduling time for yourself as you would an important meeting, even if it's just for a few minutes, to foster self-reflection and mindfulness.
key_insights.quote: If you look at your schedule, you would never cancel an important meeting with someone else, but we don't even schedule one with ourself.

key_insights.insight: Breath control is a fundamental skill, applicable to athletes, musicians, and everyday life, for managing emotions and optimizing performance.
key_insights.quote: Our breath is connected to every single emotion. So if we want to navigate our emotions and our life, we have to train our breath.

key_insights.insight: Most people are not breathing optimally and therefore, missing out on the opportunity to control their emotions through breathework.
key_insights.quote: I am saying that most people don't know how to breathe. And I didn't know how to breathe until I was taught how to breathe.

key_insights.insight: Consciously altering your breathing patterns can affect your energy levels, helping you to relax or energize depending on the desired outcome.
key_insights.quote: If you're breathing in for four, breathe out for more than four to relax and rest your body. And if you're one of these people that goes... I need more energy. Breathe out for less time than you breathe in.

key_insights.insight: Modern life often triggers a chronic 'fight or flight' response due to overstimulation, leading to heightened anxiety and stress.
key_insights.quote: We pretty much live most of our lives these days because of the overstimulation because of the stress because of the screens in this kind of like permanent state of fight or flight.

actionable_advice.advice: Schedule time for yourself in your calendar.
actionable_advice.how_to: Block out 5 minutes a day (or even 2 if that's all you can manage) in your calendar as 'me time' and stick to it as you would an important meeting.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice breathing in for four counts and out for four counts to regulate emotions.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take 2-3 minutes to breathe in and out, focusing on a count of four for both inhalation and exhalation. Do this regularly.

actionable_advice.advice: Adjust your breathing pattern to promote relaxation or increase energy.
actionable_advice.how_to: To relax, breathe out for longer than you breathe in (e.g., in for 4, out for 6). To energize, breathe out for less time than you breathe in, with a sharp exhalation.

memorable_stories: Story about breathwork coach in Indonesia explaining fight or flight
contrarian_claims: Most people don't know how to breathe correctly, even though it's a fundamental physiological process.
Scheduling time with oneself is often neglected yet crucial for well-being.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 60 - Jay Shetty: You’re Breathing Wrong, Here’s Why
